visual_findings.control_map_diagnostic: The diagnostic image is the expected black-background OpenPose skeleton map with colored limb segments and white facial/keypoint markers. It matches the intended pose-map role and shows no unexpected photographic content.
visual_findings.face_realism: Pass. The generated portrait has coherent facial structure, believable skin tone, aligned eyes, stable nose and mouth anatomy, and no obvious facial distortion.
visual_findings.eye_quality: Pass. Eyes are clear and aligned with believable catchlights and no cross-eye artifact.
visual_findings.skin_texture: Pass with notes. Skin has visible stubble and some pore detail, but still trends slightly polished in forehead and cheek regions.
visual_findings.hair_realism: Pass. Hair shape and strand mass are coherent with plausible highlights and no obvious melting.
visual_findings.pose_and_anatomy: Pass for the visible head-and-shoulders crop. Neck, shoulders, shirt collar, and blazer alignment are coherent; hands and full-body pose are not visible and therefore not certified.
visual_findings.clothing_and_fabric: Pass. Blazer, shirt, and tie are realistic enough for a local smoke sample with stable collar boundaries.
visual_findings.background_lighting_camera: Pass. Background and lighting are coherent, portrait-style, and free of visible pose-map contamination.
visual_findings.control_map_leakage: Pass. No visible OpenPose colored bones, dots, skeleton overlays, or map stains appear in the generated portrait.
visual_findings.prompt_compliance: Pass with notes. The output is a hyperrealistic editorial head-and-shoulders portrait with natural posture, but the test does not exercise hands or wider pose control.

notes: This proves local OpenPose ControlNet model load and bounded generation for one head-and-shoulders sample.
The sample does not certify hands, full-body pose, multi-person pose, or broader pose robustness.
Slight polished skin remains as a quality note.
promotion_boundary: Local smoke generation plus strict visual QA only. Final OpenPose certification and target-runtime EC2 proof remain unclaimed.
next_action: Use this as first local OpenPose generation proof. For OpenPose certification, run broader pose/robustness samples including hands or full-body framing; otherwise continue local-first with the remaining normal ControlNet model proof.
